reasoning: Exchange rates fundamentally approximate a random walk, placing the base rate at 0.50. However, both technical and fundamental factors currently point to a stronger Korean Won (meaning a lower USD/KRW exchange rate). The FRED linear trend over the prior month is downward (-0.63 won per day), and the spot rate has dropped from over 1470 in December to roughly 1440 by late February. Fundamentally, analysts from institutions like ING and Bank of America have recently revised their medium-term forecasts to predict continued KRW strengthening towards the 1300-1400 range in H1 2026. A major catalyst for this expected capital inflow is South Korea's scheduled inclusion in the World Government Bond Index (WGBI) in April 2026. Given the expected downward drift in the USD/KRW rate, the likelihood of an increase is less than 50%. Following the strict requirement that uncertainty must increase for distant dates (moving probabilities closer to 0.5), I have set the shorter-term March 8 forecast slightly lower at 44%, and the longer-term March 31 forecast closer to the random walk baseline at 47%.
